assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Atomic recreation: Each 256-chip TPU slice is recreated entirely in a single step — not rolling' and emphasizes this concept multiple times

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Cascading restart: A maintenance event on ANY slice triggers restart of ALL 4 slices simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All pods restart: Every pod across all 1,024 TPU chips will terminate and restart' and describes 'complete interruption'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response states 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices block until hardware is available' and mentions 'Hours if TPU v5e capacity is constrained during recreation'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes specific command 'kubectl exec -it TRAINING_POD -- python save_checkpoint.py --force' under 'Before maintenance: Force checkpoint save'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes checklist item 'Configure training framework for automatic resumption from latest checkpoint' and provides specific resumption commands

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed gcloud commands for applying maintenance exclusions and mentions 'Apply no upgrades maintenance exclusion to defer 30 days if needed'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response includes checklist item 'Schedule maintenance during natural training gap if possible' and suggests 'Plan training campaigns in <30 day cycles with natural maintenance gaps'
